evals.prompt: Run a quick SEO check on https://example.com
evals.expected_output: Should invoke lighthouse_audit or mcp-seo lighthouse to get an overall SEO score. Should report the score and top issues from the tool output. Should not attempt to manually inspect HTML or guess issues.
assertions: Uses lighthouse_audit MCP tool or mcp-seo lighthouse CLI command
Reports the overall score from tool output
Identifies top issues by category
Does not guess SEO issues without running tools

evals.prompt: Analyze the meta tags and heading structure of https://example.com
evals.expected_output: Should invoke analyze_meta_tags and analyze_headings as two separate tool calls. Should report title, description, OG tags, canonical from meta analysis, and H1-H6 hierarchy from headings analysis.
assertions: Uses analyze_meta_tags MCP tool or mcp-seo meta CLI command
Uses analyze_headings MCP tool or mcp-seo headings CLI command
Reports title tag and meta description findings
Reports heading hierarchy findings
Makes both tool calls rather than guessing from HTML

evals.prompt: Do a full SEO audit of https://example.com including performance data
evals.expected_output: Should invoke full_seo_report for the comprehensive analysis. Should also run analyze_performance and analyze_mobile separately as recommended by the comprehensive audit workflow. Should present findings organized by category.
assertions: Uses full_seo_report MCP tool or mcp-seo report CLI command
Uses analyze_performance for Core Web Vitals
Uses analyze_mobile for mobile-friendliness
Follows the Comprehensive Audit workflow
Presents findings organized by category with priorities

evals.prompt: Compare the SEO of https://example.com and https://competitor.com
evals.expected_output: Should run the same analysis tools on both URLs and compare results. Should follow the Competitive Analysis workflow: analyze_meta_tags, analyze_content, analyze_structured_data, and analyze_headings on each URL.
assertions: Runs the same tools on both URLs
Uses analyze_meta_tags on both URLs
Uses analyze_content on both URLs
Compares results side by side
Follows the Competitive Analysis workflow

evals.prompt: Check if https://example.com has any crawlability issues
evals.expected_output: Should invoke analyze_robots, analyze_sitemap, and analyze_headers to check crawling configuration. Should report robots.txt rules, sitemap health, and any redirect or blocking issues.
assertions: Uses analyze_robots to check robots.txt
Uses analyze_sitemap to check sitemap health
Uses analyze_headers to check redirects and security
Reports crawl-blocking issues if found
Checks sitemap references in robots.txt

evals.prompt: Check the structured data on https://example.com
evals.expected_output: Should invoke analyze_structured_data (not web_fetch or curl). Should report found JSON-LD, Microdata, and RDFa schemas. Should note that this tool renders the page with a headless browser for accurate detection.
assertions: Uses analyze_structured_data MCP tool or mcp-seo structured-data CLI
Does NOT rely on web_fetch or curl for schema detection
Reports JSON-LD, Microdata, and RDFa findings
Provides specific schema types found

evals.prompt: The site https://example.com loads very slowly on mobile. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Should invoke analyze_performance and analyze_mobile. Should report Core Web Vitals (TTFB, FCP, LCP, CLS, TBT) and compare against thresholds. Should identify mobile-specific issues like viewport, font sizes, tap targets.
assertions: Uses analyze_performance for Core Web Vitals
Uses analyze_mobile for mobile-specific issues
Reports specific metrics (TTFB, FCP, LCP, CLS)
Compares metrics against thresholds (LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1)
Identifies specific causes of slowness from tool output

evals.prompt: Crawl https://example.com and find any duplicate content across the site
evals.expected_output: Should invoke crawl_site to perform a multi-page crawl with cross-page duplicate detection. Should report duplicate titles, descriptions, and content found across pages.
assertions: Uses crawl_site MCP tool or mcp-seo crawl-site CLI command
Reports cross-page duplicate content findings
Reports duplicate titles and descriptions
Provides specific URLs affected by duplicates
